meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics for Founders: No-Code Setup Guide
content.seo_description: Stop flying blind. Learn how to implement robust analytics without writing code. A professional guide for founders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To implement analytics without coding, leverage Antigravity to generate event-tracking hooks and Claude Code to inject them into your stack. By mapping user behavior to business KPIs early, you validate your MVP's value proposition, reduce churn, and accelerate your path to recurring revenue through data-driven feature iteration.
content.intro:
You are staring at a dashboard that shows zero signs of life, not because your product is failing, but because you are effectively flying blind. Most founders wait until they have 'real' traffic to track data, but in the brutal world of SaaS, that is a death sentence. Without a telemetry layer, you cannot distinguish between a feature that needs polishing and one that needs to be killed.

The good news? The era of needing a dedicated data engineer to instrument your app is over. By leveraging AI-assisted workflows, you can turn your MVP into an insights engine in an afternoon. This guide strips away the technical complexity, allowing you to focus on the only metric that matters: growth.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before touching code, identify the single user action that proves your product provides value. This prevents 'vanity metric' bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Growth Lead. Identify the top 3 event triggers for a B2B SaaS dashboard that indicate activation and retention based on a standard onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze my current project structure and identify the best files to inject event-tracking hooks for user onboarding.'

steps.title: Provision the Analytics SDK
steps.description: Select a provider like PostHog or Mixpanel and initialize the client. We want minimal footprint and maximum reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable initialization script for a PostHog analytics client, including error handling and environment variable integration for API keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install the posthog-js dependency and create a config file at /src/lib/analytics.ts'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Actions
steps.description: Wrap your primary call-to-action buttons with tracking event calls. This is where you map user intent to your business funnel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper function for tracking events that automatically includes timestamp, user_id, and session_metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find all primary CTA buttons in the components folder and wrap the onClick handlers with the trackEvent function'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Session replays allow you to watch users struggle, giving you a 'fly on the wall' view of your UX friction points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a configuration snippet for enabling session recording, ensuring PII masking is applied to sensitive input fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add the session recording init script to the main layout component, ensuring it only runs in production environment'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Connect your analytics provider to a dashboard view so you can monitor progress in real-time without manual database queries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a SQL query snippet to aggregate daily active users (DAU) and conversion rate from the sign-up page to the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a simple dashboard view that renders the top 3 KPIs using the data captured by our new analytics layer'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you measure 50 events, you'll analyze none. Focus on 3-5 conversion events that correlate with revenue.
Always use a development-to-production environment variable toggle for your analytics keys to prevent test data from polluting your real metrics.
Use 'Property Mapping' to attach user traits (like plan tier or signup date) to every event; it makes segmenting your data infinitely easier later.
Implement 'Autocapture' with caution; it creates data noise. Curated, manual event tracking is always superior for high-intent SaaS funnels.
Set up Slack alerts for your 'North Star' event. Seeing a notification every time a user hits a milestone is the best motivation for a solo founder.
content.next_steps: Once your tracking is live, wait for 50-100 user sessions to populate your dashboard. Use the data to identify the 'drop-off' point in your funnel and run an A/B test on that specific component using your newfound insights.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing what your users want and start building exactly what they need to pay you for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
